title: data-us shared provenance + error envelope
description: Reusable definitions for tier-aware provenance metadata and partial-failure error wrappers used across all data-us pack outputs. Referenced via $ref by the per-pack schemas.

provenance.description: Source-tier metadata attached to fetched payloads. Tier A = official primary source (SEC EDGAR, FRED). Tier 2 = unofficial/scraper (yfinance). tier_1 / tier_2 / 2-gap are alternate spellings used by various clients; consumers should accept all five strings.

source.description: Free-form source label, e.g. 'yfinance (unofficial Yahoo Finance scraper)', 'SEC EDGAR (data.sec.gov)', 'FRED CSV (fred.stlouisfed.org)'.

source_authority.description: Issuing institution. Common values: 'U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission', 'Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis', 'Yahoo Finance (unofficial, not endorsed)'.

data_type.description: Common values: 'official_regulatory_filing' (SEC filings), 'official_macro_indicator' / 'official_government_statistics' (FRED — both seen in the wild; the latter used by FRED preset bundles), 'unofficial_scraper' (yfinance).

update_cycle.description: Free-form description of the upstream update cadence (e.g. 'near-real-time (15 min delay)', 'filing-driven', 'daily / weekly / monthly').

typical_lag.description: Human-readable typical publication lag (e.g. '15 minutes (price) / 1 day (fundamentals)', '0-60 days depending on form').

fetched_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at fetch time (UTC, second precision typical).

reference_period.description: Period that the fetched payload describes (e.g. observation date for FRED, latest filing date for SEC). Null when not meaningful.

staleness_days.description: Days between reference_period and fetched_at; null when reference_period is null.

tier.description: Coarse tier classification. 'A' / 'tier_1' = official primary source. '2' / 'tier_2' = unofficial fallback. '2-gap' = Tier 2 used because Tier A is unavailable for this field.

tier_label.description: Human-readable expansion of `tier`, e.g. 'Tier A (SEC EDGAR official)' or 'Tier 2 (yfinance unofficial)'.

upgrade_hint.description: When tier is '2' / '2-gap' / 'tier_2': URL or instruction to upgrade to a Tier A source (e.g. 'Use SEC EDGAR for verdict-grade fundamentals').

error_wrapper.description: Replaces a payload slot when the underlying client subprocess failed (non-zero exit, JSON parse error, timeout). The pack itself still exits 0; consumers detect failure by `_error` (or `error`) key presence. Both spellings appear in real output: top-level packs use `error`, nested slots can carry `_error`.

args.description: Argv passed to the failing client.

stderr.description: Truncated stderr (last 2000 chars) from the failing subprocess.

_cmd.description: Failed subprocess argv list (for reproducibility); string form may appear when the failure is at the pack-orchestration level.

detail.description: Free-form error detail (e.g. JSON parse exception message).

stdout_head.description: First 500 chars of stdout when JSON parse failed.

_partial.description: True when the pack succeeded overall but one or more nested slots are error wrappers (partial-failure semantics).
